name: Workbench Semantic Projection Proof Gate
purpose: Produce direct Module-owned Evidence that Kernel and Workbench projections preserve exact authoring and action semantics without adapter-side governance compilation.

oracle.description: The fixed Project Model module Claim/Gate projection proof entrypoint must exist, prove the bounded batch Interface, and exit zero.

rejects: publicContracts or dependency via Contract Claims omitted, invented, duplicated, or exposed to an unrelated Module
frozen-Plan target or cross-source route selection that broadens authoring Claim visibility
a requested Module and Claim pair with no acceptance route omitted instead of retained as an empty route list
acceptance routes produced by consumer-side Gate scope, command scope, or effective Module filtering
integration- or release-only full-Gate expansion admitted as an acceptance route
route shape, order, or canonical digest rewritten by the module projection
forged, wrong-Model, incomplete, drifted, serialized, or aggregate-budget-exceeding route-product input

residualUncertainty: Stable composite source orchestration and lifecycle action policy remain Change Kernel responsibilities.
Local Workbench projection and browser adoption are proved separately.

oracle.description: The fixed Change Kernel Workbench semantics test entrypoint must exist, prove the Kernel-owned projection, and exit zero.

rejects: selectable Claims outside a primary Module's owned or dependency Contracts
omitted, duplicated, or invented authoring Claims or acceptance-eligible routes
authoring routes that differ from the current Architecture Profile route product
Change Gate actions derived from current browser Project facts instead of the frozen Governance Baseline, Kernel lifecycle selection, and compiler-owned Claim-route annotations
omitted, duplicated, or invented existing-Change Gate actions
unavailable actions with an empty, duplicate, unknown, or out-of-precedence disabled-reason code list

residualUncertainty: Local Workbench projection and real adapter adoption are proved by a separate Module-owned command.
Browser DOM interaction remains outside this Gate and belongs to LGT-014.

oracle.description: The fixed Change Kernel adoption proof entrypoint must exist, prove exclusive consumption of Project Model module Claim/Gate projections, and exit zero.

rejects: Kernel traversal of Contract ownership or dependency visibility for Workbench authoring Claims
Kernel interpretation of Gate scope, command scope, or effective Module applicability
current or frozen-Plan acceptance routes obtained outside Project Model's bounded batch projection
loss of Kernel ownership over stable source orchestration, lifecycle policy, disabled-reason ordering, or compiler-bound Verification Plan validation

residualUncertainty: Local Workbench projection and browser adoption are proved by a separate Module-owned command.
The proof covers one local single-writer stable query; multi-writer history remains outside the current assurance boundary.

oracle.description: The fixed Local Workbench profile test entrypoint must prove pure projection of Kernel semantics and exit zero.

rejects: loss or invention of selectable Claims, exact Gate and command routes, enabled state, or declared workbenchDisabledReasonCodes
omission or duplication of canonical Kernel authoring or action facts
CLI, HTTP, or browser adapter reconstruction of Contract visibility, Gate applicability, or lifecycle policy
aggregate score, percentage, confidence, health, readiness, or green-light conclusions
top-level view payloads containing source bodies, package bodies, Evidence bodies, stdout, or stderr instead of bounded references and digests

residualUncertainty: Browser DOM rendering and interaction remain outside this Gate and belong to LGT-014.
Cross-workspace Accepted history portability remains an explicit Knowledge Gap.

command: const proof = await import('./test/change-kernel/workbench-semantics.test.mjs'); if (proof.WORKBENCH_INPUT_REQUIREMENTS_PROOF_VERSION !== 1) throw new Error('The fixed Change Kernel Workbench input-requirements proof is not implemented.');

oracle.description: The fixed Change Kernel Workbench semantics module must export proof version 1 only after compiler- and validator-owned input requirements are exercised.

rejects: Plan Outcome eligibility or cardinality derived outside the compiler seam that enforces Change planRefs
acceptance-input requirements derived outside the lifecycle validators that enforce Closure, authority, Decision, scope, amendment, adoption, obligation, and confirmation semantics
omitted, invented, duplicate, unbounded, or cross-source Workbench input requirements
a selected Change whose requirements are compiled from current facts instead of its frozen Governance Baseline and one stabilized source query

residualUncertainty: The proof covers one local single-writer stabilized Workbench query; authenticated identity and multi-writer coordination remain outside the current boundary.
Local Workbench projection and browser submission are proved separately.

command: const proof = await import('./test/local-workbench/architecture-profile.test.mjs'); if (proof.WORKBENCH_INPUT_REQUIREMENTS_PROOF_VERSION !== 1) throw new Error('The fixed Local Workbench input-requirements proof is not implemented.');

oracle.description: The fixed Local Workbench Profile module must export proof version 1 only after its adapter and DOM input-requirement scenario is exercised.

rejects: browser filtering of raw Plan facts or invention of Closure, authority, Decision, amendment, adoption, obligation, or confirmation policy
loss, duplication, invention, or cross-source replacement of Kernel-declared authoring and acceptance-input requirements
submission of an Outcome, authority, Decision type, field, amendment, adoption, or Closure mode not declared by the selected source-bound projection
automatic substitution of a Decision Authority id for the independently supplied human actor

residualUncertainty: The DOM proof is semantic rather than a pixel-perfect or cross-browser visual test.
Authenticated human identity and portable Accepted history remain outside the current boundary.

command: const proof = await import('./test/change-kernel/workbench-semantics.test.mjs'); if (proof.WORKBENCH_PROJECTION_INTEGRITY_PROOF_VERSION !== 1) throw new Error('The fixed Change Kernel Workbench projection-integrity proof is not implemented.');

oracle.description: The fixed Change Kernel Workbench semantics module must export proof version 1 only after terminal source-bound Claim selection and exact Contract vocabulary parity are exercised.

rejects: raw protected Claim sets exposed to adapters or final integrity eligibility reconstructed outside Kernel
a missing, duplicate, invented, or incorrectly keyed claimSelectionRoutes entry for a Change kind, selected Outcome, Module, or visible Claim
final Claim options whose exact acceptance routes differ from the current Project Model route product
Contract disabled-reason codes or precedence that differ from the exported Kernel closed vocabulary

residualUncertainty: The proof covers one bounded local stabilized Workbench projection; multi-writer history remains outside the current boundary.
Browser content verification and exact route lookup are proved by the Local Workbench command.

command: const proof = await import('./test/local-workbench/architecture-profile.test.mjs'); if (proof.WORKBENCH_BROWSER_PROJECTION_INTEGRITY_PROOF_VERSION !== 1) throw new Error('The fixed Local Workbench browser projection-integrity proof is not implemented.');

oracle.description: The fixed Local Workbench Profile module must export proof version 1 only after browser canonical digest verification, forbidden aggregate rejection, and exact Kernel Claim-option lookup are exercised.

rejects: Workbench, Profile page, or Profile window content accepted after a stale canonical digest mutation
aggregate assurance accepted after injection and resealing at the browser boundary
browser consumption of protected Claim sets, Boolean intersection of eligibility, or invention of disabled explanations
a missing, duplicate, invented, or malformed exact Claim-selection route accepted by the receiver

residualUncertainty: SHA-256 content verification inside the loopback boundary is not a signature or remote-server authentication.
The DOM proof is semantic rather than pixel-perfect or cross-browser visual verification.
